title: Enable event logging,  tunnel and ABI mode for FreeBSD
body:
This allows podman commands on FreeBSD to call through to libpod and is required for most commands to work. Right now, most commands are non-functional but 'podman image' is working ok.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Tests are angry. Code change LGTM though.

> Tests are angry. Code change LGTM though.

The two fails both look like this:

```
[+0029s] not ok 192 [10-images] POST libpod/build?remote=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Falpinelinux%2Fdocker-alpine.git : .stream
[+0029s] #  expected: ~ STEP 1/5: FROM alpine:3.14
[+0029s] #    actual: STEP 1/5: FROM alpine:3.16
[+0029s] 
[+0029s] Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
[+0029s] 
[+0029s] Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.16...
[+0029s] 
[+0029s] null
```

Not sure what to make of that (or how my change could possiblty affect image fetch)

@dfr: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15381#issuecomment-1220884647):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@edsantiago I believe you were looking at that CI failures?
No, I only reported it (#15388). I just took a quick look at the code and couldn't figure out how to do a clean fix. So, next week.
I completely missed the part where the test was looking for alpine:3.14 but actually got alpine:3.16. I guess that accounts for the fact that no new commits have been merged for more than a day.

> No, I only reported it (#15388). I just took a quick look at the code and couldn't figure out how to do a clean fix. So, next week.

A rebase will pull in fixes.
